subject: Physics
title: Quantum Electrodynamics
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Quantum Electrodynamics.

hook.text: QED is the most precisely tested theory in all of science. It predicts experiments to 12 decimal places.

buildup.text: Quantum electrodynamics describes how light and matter interact at the quantum level.

discovery.text: Feynman, Schwinger, and Tomonaga independently developed QED in the late 1940s. All three won the Nobel.

twist.text: The theory says particles exchange virtual photons to create electromagnetic forces. Forces are conversations.

climax.text: QED's precision is like measuring the distance from New York to Los Angeles to the width of a human hair.

punchline.text: Twelve decimal places of accuracy. Physics' most precise theory.
